---
title: "Corvus Star Phase 4 Implementation Backlog"
date: 2026-03-09
status: completed
---

# Corvus Star Phase 4 Implementation Backlog

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This backlog operationalizes Phase 4 from `docs/architecture/WOVEN_SKILL_CONSOLIDATION_BLUEPRINT.qmd`.
> Phase 4 standardizes TALIESIN execution.
> TALIESIN may remain a runtime identity, but code forge can no longer bypass bead authority, contract discipline, or validation handoff.

## Phase 4 Mission

Phase 4 exists to make TALIESIN a canonical forge weave instead of a collection of direct script paths and permissive adapters.

The required outcomes are:

1. TALIESIN execution is dispatched through the runtime with an explicit forge contract.
2. Canonical forge execution requires a real bead and a real contract surface.
3. Forge output includes an explicit validation handoff instead of a raw staged file alone.
4. Transitional lore or freeform adapters stop being live forge authority.
5. Any active caller that still forges code must go through the Hall-backed bead flow and validation-ready result envelope.

## Phase 4 Exit Criteria

- A stable runtime weave exists for TALIESIN forge execution.
- Canonical forge execution rejects beads that lack contract references, acceptance criteria, or executable target identity.
- Forge staging emits an explicit validation request or equivalent structured handoff, not only a staged path.
- No live runtime or CLI forge path depends on lore-file normalization or freeform objective text as the canonical source of work.
- Transitional TALIESIN adapters are clearly compatibility-only and do not define operational authority.

## Execution Status

Current Phase 4 state:

- Phase 1 completed on 2026-03-09
- Phase 2 completed on 2026-03-09
- Phase 3 completed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P4-00` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P4-01` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P4-02` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P4-03` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P4-04` closed on 2026-03-09
- TALIESIN forge now dispatches through `weave:taliesin-forge` with a structured staged-candidate envelope
- Canonical forge requests now require an eligible bead plus explicit contract refs and acceptance criteria
- The script-level lore-file forge entrypoint is now compatibility-only and rejected by the canonical runtime path
- Target-driven `start` execution no longer falls through to the legacy agent loop or lore/objective forge path
- Contractless, workflow-only, or lore-only beads now normalize directly into triage instead of pretending to be executable work
- Forge staging now emits a Crucible-consumable validation handoff with staged candidate and generated test metadata
- Freeform JIT forge fallback now creates triage beads instead of generating code, and dead forge bypass files are quarantined
- Phase 4 completed on 2026-03-09

## Ticket Index

| ID | Status | Priority | Ticket | Depends On | Primary Surfaces |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `CS-P4-00` | Closed | P0 | TALIESIN Forge Runtime Contract Freeze | Phase 3 complete | `runtime/contracts.ts`, `runtime/bootstrap.ts`, `dispatcher.ts`, `taliesin_forge.py`, `forge_candidate.py` |
| `CS-P4-01` | Closed | P0 | Require Bead-Backed Forge Input | `CS-P4-00` | `forge_candidate.py`, `bead_ledger.py`, forge request tests |
| `CS-P4-02` | Closed | P0 | Require Contract-First Forge Input | `CS-P4-00` | `forge_candidate.py`, `taliesin_forge.py`, request validation and compatibility fences |
| `CS-P4-03` | Closed | P1 | Emit Validation Handoff From Forge | `CS-P4-00`, `CS-P4-02` | `forge_candidate.py`, Crucible handoff metadata, runtime result typing |
| `CS-P4-04` | Closed | P1 | Retire Direct Forge Bypass Paths | `CS-P4-01`, `CS-P4-02`, `CS-P4-03` | `taliesin_forge.py`, `skill_forger.py`, legacy forge callers, command adapters |

## Critical Path

The critical path is:

`CS-P4-00 -> CS-P4-01 -> CS-P4-02 -> CS-P4-03 -> CS-P4-04`

## Ticket Details

## `CS-P4-00` TALIESIN Forge Runtime Contract Freeze

### Objective

Freeze the canonical TALIESIN forge invocation and result boundary before broader enforcement and cleanup.

### Files

- `src/node/core/runtime/contracts.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/bootstrap.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/`
- `src/node/core/commands/dispatcher.ts`
- `src/types/forge-candidate.ts`
- `docs/legacy_archive/src_sentinel/taliesin_forge.py`
- `src/core/engine/forge_candidate.py`
- New or updated TALIESIN runtime tests

### Change Set

- Define a canonical `weave:taliesin-forge` invocation and metadata envelope.
- Add a runtime-backed TALIESIN forge entrypoint that accepts bead-backed parameters and returns a structured staged candidate result.
- Add a Python CLI envelope for runtime TALIESIN execution so TypeScript dispatch does not scrape freeform text.
- Demote lore-file forge invocation to compatibility-only behavior; the runtime path must be bead-first.

### Acceptance Criteria

- The runtime can dispatch TALIESIN forge through a stable weave id.
- TALIESIN forge returns a structured candidate envelope rather than ad hoc terminal text.
- No canonical runtime invocation for TALIESIN depends on a lore file path.

### Verification

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npx tsx --test tests/unit/test_runtime_dispatch.test.ts tests/unit/test_forge_candidate.test.ts tests/unit/test_taliesin_forge_runtime.test.ts
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/unit/test_forge_candidate.py -q
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- The runtime now exposes `weave:taliesin-forge` as the canonical TALIESIN forge entrypoint.
- CLI dispatch now maps `forge` into the runtime spine instead of falling through to legacy command guessing.
- TALIESIN forge now emits a structured staged candidate envelope plus explicit validation handoff metadata.
- Canonical forge request construction now rejects beads that lack contract refs, acceptance criteria, or a forge-eligible lifecycle status.
- The direct lore-file TALIESIN forge CLI path is now compatibility-only and explicitly rejected for canonical runtime execution.

## `CS-P4-01` Require Bead-Backed Forge Input

### Objective

Remove non-bead canonical execution paths so TALIESIN always starts from sovereign work units.

### Files

- `src/core/engine/forge_candidate.py`
- `docs/legacy_archive/src_sentinel/taliesin_forge.py`
- `src/node/agent_loop.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/`
- Updated caller tests

### Change Set

- Require `bead_id` for canonical forge execution.
- Reject direct target-plus-objective forge calls in any active runtime path.
- Update remaining live callers to resolve a bead before invoking TALIESIN.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Canonical forge does not mint its own work unit during live execution.
- Active callers can no longer invoke TALIESIN with only a file path and freeform objective.

### Verification

```powershell
npx tsx --test tests/unit/test_start_runtime.test.ts tests/unit/test_taliesin_forge_runtime.test.ts tests/unit/test_runtime_dispatch.test.ts tests/unit/test_runtime_command_invocations.test.ts
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/unit/test_forge_candidate.py -q
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- `weave:start` no longer executes target-driven legacy forge or agent-loop behavior.
- Target-plus-objective start calls now fail fast and direct operators toward bead-backed TALIESIN execution.
- The direct lore-to-forge helper no longer normalizes lore into a live bead during execution.

## `CS-P4-02` Require Contract-First Forge Input

### Objective

Ensure every canonical forge request is anchored to explicit contract references and acceptance criteria.

### Files

- `src/core/engine/forge_candidate.py`
- `src/core/engine/bead_ledger.py`
- `docs/legacy_archive/src_sentinel/taliesin.py`
- `docs/legacy_archive/src_sentinel/taliesin_forge.py`
- Updated request validation tests

### Change Set

- Reject forge requests that lack contract references or acceptance criteria.
- Distinguish narrative lore ingestion from code forge authority.
- Mark missing-contract beads as triage material, not executable forge work.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Canonical TALIESIN forge fails fast on contractless or criteria-free beads.
- Lore and style surfaces no longer masquerade as contract input for code change execution.

### Verification

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/unit/test_bead_ledger.py tests/unit/test_forge_candidate.py tests/unit/test_mission_coordinator.py tests/unit/test_rpc_projection.py tests/unit/test_muninn_promotion.py tests/contracts/test_muninn.py tests/empire_tests/test_norn_campaign_empire.py tests/empire_tests/test_norn_empire.py -q
npx tsx --test tests/unit/test_start_runtime.test.ts tests/unit/test_taliesin_forge_runtime.test.ts
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- Bead actionability now requires target identity, acceptance criteria, and executable contract references.
- New or normalized beads with only lore, workflow, or registry refs now land in `NEEDS_TRIAGE` immediately.
- Forge request builders now reject lore-only and workflow-only request sources as non-canonical contract authority.

## `CS-P4-03` Emit Validation Handoff From Forge

### Objective

Make forge output explicitly consumable by Crucible and promotion stages.

### Files

- `src/core/engine/forge_candidate.py`
- `src/types/forge-candidate.ts`
- `src/core/engine/ravens/muninn_crucible.py`
- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/`
- Updated validation handoff tests

### Change Set

- Add explicit validation request metadata to staged candidate results.
- Ensure the forge result carries bead id, target path, contract refs, required validations, and acceptance criteria into the validation boundary.
- Remove any caller assumption that a staged file alone is enough to proceed.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Forge output is validation-ready as a structured envelope.
- Crucible-facing callers no longer have to infer validation scope from a staged path alone.

### Verification

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/unit/test_forge_candidate.py tests/unit/test_muninn_crucible_stage.py tests/unit/test_muninn_promotion.py -q
npx tsx --test tests/unit/test_forge_candidate.test.ts tests/unit/test_taliesin_forge_runtime.test.ts
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- Forge validation requests now carry staged candidate paths and generated test metadata.
- Crucible can normalize a forge validation handoff into a structured validation target.
- Validation execution now consumes staged candidates directly when a canonical forge handoff is present.

## `CS-P4-04` Retire Direct Forge Bypass Paths

### Objective

Collapse or quarantine the remaining TALIESIN and forge entrypoints that bypass runtime discipline.

### Files

- `docs/legacy_archive/src_sentinel/taliesin_forge.py`
- `src/core/engine/skill_forger.py`
- `src/core/engine/executor.py`
- `src/node/agent_loop.ts`
- Any remaining forge-specific compatibility tests

### Change Set

- Quarantine or excise direct lore-driven forge entrypoints that remain outside runtime control.
- Demote freeform JIT skill forging to explicit compatibility or archive status if it cannot satisfy Phase 4 discipline.
- Leave only runtime-dispatched or explicitly marked compatibility TALIESIN execution paths.

### Acceptance Criteria

- No live forge caller bypasses runtime dispatch, bead authority, and validation handoff.
- Transitional adapters are visibly compatibility-only or removed.

### Verification

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npx tsx --test tests/unit/test_start_runtime.test.ts tests/unit/test_taliesin_forge_runtime.test.ts tests/unit/test_runtime_dispatch.test.ts tests/unit/test_runtime_command_invocations.test.ts tests/unit/test_forge_candidate.test.ts
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/unit/test_executor.py tests/unit/test_forge_candidate.py tests/unit/test_bead_ledger.py tests/unit/test_muninn_crucible_stage.py tests/unit/test_muninn_promotion.py tests/contracts/test_muninn.py -q
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- The low-confidence executor fallback now captures a triage bead instead of minting or forging code directly.
- The external MCP TALIESIN tool now requires a canonical bead id instead of lore-plus-objective input.
- Legacy direct forge surfaces in `src/node/agent_loop.ts` and `src/core/engine/skill_forger.py` are quarantined alongside their dedicated tests.
